data.title: Global market for PFAS-Free EV battery fire protection coatings to reach $7.7 billion by 2036, a crucial change for electric vehicle safety.
data.description: In Newark, Delaware on December 12, 2025, it has been observed that there is a significant shift occurring in the automotive supply chain due to the increasing popularity of electric mobility worldwide. An essential safety concern related to battery fire protection, particularly the absence of PFAS, is becoming a key focus. Recent data highlights that there is a global effort towards implementing safer practices, with a growing emphasis on eliminating PFAS from battery fire protection measures. This change is not solely driven by regulatory obligations but also by the increasing demand for safer electric vehicle technology.

data.title: Whataburger offers 12 Days of Daily Deals for Rewards Members in the Whataburger App this holiday season.
data.description: Starting on December 12, 2025, Whataburger® Rewards Members will have the opportunity to enjoy a special daily deal exclusively through the Whataburger App. This promotion aims to bring joy and satisfaction to customers during the holiday season in San Antonio.

data.title: Alkami launches Foundry hub on alkami.com for technology builders, providing visibility into platform, engineering practices, and partner ecosystem. (Characters: 127)
data.description: Alkami Technology, Inc. (Nasdaq: ALKT) based in PLANO, Texas, announced on December 12, 2025, the launch of a new central resource on alkami.com. This platform aims to provide builders with enhanced insights into Alkami's Platform, engineering practices, and partner ecosystem. ALKT is a leading digital sales and service platform provider.

data.title: 2025-2026 holiday season explores new cheese trends with European touch by Granlatte, largest Italian milk coop.
data.description: In New York on December 12, 2025, the upcoming holiday season of 2025-2026 presents a unique opportunity to delve into fresh cheeseboard trends with a European flair. Granlatte, Italy's largest milk producers' cooperative, is spearheading the European Cheese Quality campaign, aiming to showcase a diverse range of cheeses to consumers. As part of this initiative, Granlatte will highlight premium European cheeses renowned for their taste and quality. The campaign aims to introduce American consumers to a wide variety of European cheese options and educate them on the distinct flavors and textures. Overall, the campaign aims to reach a broad audience of cheese enthusiasts and increase awareness of European cheese varieties in the United States.

data.title: Andersen Corp. distributed $52.2M in profit sharing and $5M in community support in 2025. #Bayport #AndersenCorporation
data.description: Andersen Corporation, based in Bayport, Minnesota, revealed on December 12, 2025, that it has disbursed over $52.2 million in profit sharing to qualifying employees for the year. Andersen, recognized as the top brand of windows and doors in the United States, highlighted its commitment to rewarding its workforce.

data.title: Incubeta names Amy Crowther as President of Americas, set to become CEO in 2026. Company focuses on AI marketing, acquires RocketSource.
data.description: In a recent announcement, global digital marketing leader Incubeta appointed Amy Crowther as President, Americas, marking the beginning of her journey towards becoming the Americas CEO by 2026. This move aligns with Incubeta's growth strategy as they focus on solidifying their position following the strategic acquisition of RocketSource. With a strong emphasis on AI-driven marketing, Incubeta aims to empower brands to excel in their marketing efforts. Crowther, who previously held the position of Chief Client, is set to lead the company into a new era of success.

data.title: Cathy Rowe of Wolters Kluwer named to Accounting Today’s 2025 Top 100 Most Influential in Accounting list.
data.description: In Minneapolis, Wolters Kluwer Tax and Accounting (TAA) revealed that Cathy Rowe, the Senior Vice President and Segment Leader for the U.S. Professional Market, has been recognized in Accounting Today's 2025 "Top 100 Most Influential People in Accounting" list for the second year running. This prestigious list highlights individuals shaping the accounting sector, and this year's selection features a diverse range of professionals including firm executives, educators, and technology innovators. Congratulations to Cathy Rowe for this well-deserved recognition for her contributions to the industry.

data.title: Bitcoin's drop from $120,000 to $90,000 driven by macro factors: risk appetite, liquidity shifts, and interest rates.
data.description: Bitcoin's (CRYPTO: BTC) current stagnant trading and a 12% decline preventing it from surpassing $100,000 have traders wondering about the reasons behind this weakness. Capital Flows, a pseudonymous macro researcher, explained in a detailed report that three major macro factors are influencing Bitcoin's downturn. Firstly, Bitcoin is functioning as a release valve for risk assets, as it is highly responsive to shifts in risk appetite. Although it usually mirrors equities, it has recently moved independently due to its quicker reaction to changes in macro liquidity and overall risk assessments. Secondly, real interest rates have taken precedence in dictating Bitcoin's price movements, despite the actions of the Federal Reserve. For a more thorough analysis, please refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: US economy looks strong by end of 2025. GDPNow projects 3.6% growth in Q3. Wall Street predicts more growth in 2026. Contrarian bets to consider.
data.description: By the end of 2025, the U.S. economy is thriving with strong economic growth and low inflation. The Federal Reserve has cut rates three times, and stock markets are at record highs. The iShares Russell 2000 ETF is approaching resistance levels. The Atlanta Fed's GDPNow predicts 3.6% growth for Q3 2025, with inflation at 2.8% and unemployment at historic lows. Despite fears of trade tariffs and AI-led job losses not materializing, the Fed has raised growth expectations for December. The market is optimistic for 2026, expecting more growth, fewer rate cuts, and higher stock prices, though concerns arise about extreme optimism leading to potential risks. Benzinga explores contrarian bets for 2026, including a potential U.S. recession. Investors could benefit from scenarios that are currently being overlooked.

data.title: AI diagnostics in point-of-care infectious disease valued at $53B in 2024, expected to double by 2033, reducing hospital costs.
data.description: Ventripoint Diagnostics Ltd., based in Vancouver, BC, announced on Dec. 12, 2025, that the point-of-care infectious disease diagnostics market, with a value of $53 billion in 2024, is expected to almost double by 2033. This growth is attributed to AI systems that enhance diagnostic processes.

data.title: Mohammed Ben Sulayem re-elected as FIA President for a second four-year term after General Assembly election in Tashkent.
data.description: In Paris, it has been officially announced that Mohammed Ben Sulayem has been re-elected as the President of the Fédération Internationale de l’Automobile (FIA), the worldwide governing body for motor sport and mobility organizations. This decision came after his Presidential List was elected by the General Assembly in Tashkent, Uzbekistan. This marks the beginning of Mohammed Ben Sulayem's second four-year term. During his tenure, he has been credited with overseeing a period of notable revitalization and stability within the organization.
